topic: ADR-002 MCP-first Rule Distribution rules architecture
limitations: fab_get_rules could not load local Fabric context because .fabric/agents.meta.json is missing in this checkout.

relevant_files.summary: Records ADR-002 and adjacent decisions for rule selection, stable identity, MCP transport, Dashboard consumer boundaries.
Defines AgentsMetaNode fields including priority, activation, stable_id, identity_source, derived defaults.
Implements stale detection, rule node matching, priority sort, activation behavior, description stubs, and human-lock injection.
Defines fab_get_rules input/output schema for single target file rule lookup.
Builds batch per-path results plus shared bundle, file map, description stub union, preflight diagnostics.
Defines fab_plan_context output schema for multi-path planning/architecture review.
Reads .fabric/human-lock.json, calculates drift/current_hash, and returns HumanLockStatus.
Defines human lock entry shape: file, start_line, end_line, hash.
Applies add/remove/update operations and computes revision for MCP registry mutations.
Computes agents.meta.json from rule files, stable identity comments, hashes, and revision source.
Combines AgentsMeta and RulesContextPayload to display hit reasons, activation tiers, and description stubs.

patterns.pattern: Source of Truth -> Resolution Service -> Delivery Contract
patterns.description: Rules are declared in meta/source files, resolved centrally in server services, then delivered through MCP/HTTP payloads.
fab_get_rules is the final per-target lookup; fab_plan_context is a batch planning read model with shared bundle output.
Dashboard consumes REST read models and recombines meta plus context for visualization.

constraints: MCP-first rule delivery keeps client prompts from becoming separate compiled truth sources.
Legacy nodes without activation default to path-based matching.
Human-lock is stateful protection data, not rule metadata.
Description stubs intentionally avoid reading full backing rule content.

risks.impact: Could add context noise at larger rule scale unless documented as global semantic candidate list.

risks.risk: revision hash algorithms differ between sync-meta and update-registry
risks.impact: Some registry metadata changes may have weaker stale semantics depending on mutation path.

risks.risk: fab_update_registry input schema does not expose activation/stable_id/identity_source
risks.impact: New registry fields are readable but not fully maintainable through the recommended MCP mutation tool.

risks.risk: human_locked_nearby naming does not match all-entry behavior
risks.impact: Clients may assume path proximity filtering that is not implemented.

key_findings: ADR-002 is directionally sound because centralized rule resolution avoids cross-client behavior drift.
The architecture should be reviewed as a set of object boundaries, not as a flat list of fields.
priority and activation belong to registry metadata; description_stubs and human_locked_nearby are derived payload views.
The most important follow-up is clarifying description tier semantics and unifying registry mutation/revision behavior.
